title: cineraria lyratiformis
intro: Cineraria lyratiformis, also known as the African cornflower or Cape marigold, is a flowering plant species native to South Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Cineraria lyratiformis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various skin conditions and infections. Cineraria lyratiformis is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
